query: One Exposome – the Human Exposome meets the One Health Paradigm

t1.1.name: Exposome Domains and Classification
t1.1.description: The three traditional exposome domains: internal, specific external, and general external environments, including chemical, physical, biological, and social exposure factors.

t1.name: Exposome Concept and Framework
t1.description: Foundational understanding of the exposome paradigm, including its definition, evolution, and methodological approaches for measuring total environmental exposures across the lifespan.

t2.1.name: Human-Animal-Environment Interface
t2.1.description: The critical connections and shared exposure pathways between humans, animals, and ecosystems, including shared pathogens, antimicrobial resistance, and environmental contaminants affecting multiple species.

t2.name: One Health Paradigm Integration
t2.description: The One Health approach connecting human, animal, and environmental health, and its integration with exposome science to address interconnected health challenges at the human-animal-environment interface.

t3.name: One Exposome Conceptual Framework
t3.description: The synthesis of exposome science with One Health principles, creating an integrated framework that examines environmental exposures across humans, animals, and ecosystems simultaneously to understand shared health determinants.

t1.1.name: One Health Implementation Models
t1.1.description: Operational frameworks and case studies demonstrating how One Health principles are applied across different sectors including zoonotic disease surveillance, antimicrobial resistance programs, and ecosystem health initiatives.

t1.name: One Health Paradigm Foundations
t1.description: Fundamental concepts of the One Health approach, emphasizing interconnections between human, animal, and environmental health as a unified framework for addressing complex health challenges.

t2.1.name: Exposome Measurement Technologies
t2.1.description: Advanced technologies and methodologies for measuring and monitoring exposome components, including personal sensors, omics technologies, geospatial tracking, and integrated data platforms.

t2.name: Human Exposome Concept and Assessment
t2.description: Comprehensive characterization of the human exposome, encompassing all environmental exposures from conception onwards, including chemical, physical, biological, and social factors that influence health outcomes.

t3.1.name: Shared Environmental Stressors
t3.1.description: Common environmental exposures affecting human, animal, and ecosystem health simultaneously, including air pollution, water contaminants, climate change impacts, and chemical pollutants.

t3.2.name: Policy and Governance Implications
t3.2.description: Regulatory frameworks, policy development, and governance structures needed to operationalize the One Exposome approach across health, environmental, and agricultural sectors.

t3.name: Integration of Exposome and One Health
t3.description: Conceptual and practical integration of exposome science with One Health principles, creating a unified framework that addresses environmental exposures across human, animal, and ecosystem boundaries.

t1.1.description: Core principles of exposome science including the totality of environmental exposures from conception onwards, internal and external exposome components, and temporal dynamics of exposure assessment.

t1.2.description: Development and core tenets of the One Health approach emphasizing interconnections between human, animal, and environmental health, including zoonotic disease interfaces and ecosystem health considerations.

t1.3.description: Justification and theoretical basis for merging exposome and One Health frameworks, identifying synergies, complementary approaches, and enhanced understanding of complex health determinants across domains.
